ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "original_title" TEXT /* The original title of the movie */,
  "overview" TEXT /* A brief synopsis of the movie */,
  "budget" TEXT /* The budget allocated for the movie */,
  "revenue" TEXT /* The revenue generated by the movie */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* The release date of the movie */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* The total runtime of the movie in minutes */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the movie (e.g., Released, Unreleased) */,
  "tagline" TEXT /* Tagline associated with the movie */,
  "homepage" TEXT /* Homepage URL for the movie */,
  "production_companies" TEXT /* Production companies involved in the movie */,
  "production_countries" TEXT /* Countries where the movie was produced */,
  "spoken_languages" TEXT /* Languages spoken in the movie */,
  "imdb_id" TEXT /* IMDb ID of the movie */,
  "tmdb_id" TEXT /* TMDb ID of the movie */,
  "poster_path" TEXT /* Path to the movie's poster image */,
  "backdrop_path" TEXT /* Path to the movie's backdrop image */,
  "is_adult" INTEGER /* Whether the movie is adult content */,
  "popularity" TEXT /* Popularity score of the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the genre */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the genre (e.g., Comedy, Drama) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "collections" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the collection */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the collection */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the collection */,
  "parent_collection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent collection (if any) */,
  "is_visible" INTEGER /* Whether the collection is visible to all users */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_collections" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "collection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the collection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "collection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the language */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code of the language */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_languages" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "language_id" INTEGER /* ID of the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being rated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who rated the movie */,
  "rating" REAL /* Rating given by the user */,
  "vote_count" INTEGER /* Number of votes for the rating */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the rating was given */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the rating was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the system */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date the user joined the platform */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the review */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being reviewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who wrote the review */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the review */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the review was created */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the review */,
  "is_spoiler" INTEGER /* Whether the review contains spoilers */,
  "helpful_votes" INTEGER /* Number of users who found the review helpful */,
  "not_helpful_votes" INTEGER /* Number of users who did not find the review helpful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the movie */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the movie was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, search) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device_info" TEXT /* Information about the device used to access the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_crew" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the crew member */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie they worked on */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the crew member */,
  "job" TEXT /* Job title of the crew member */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department they worked in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_crew_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "awards" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the award */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie that won the award */,
  "award_name" TEXT /* Name of the award */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the award was given */,
  "won" INTEGER /* Whether the movie won the award */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_awards_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("id")
)
